rulings.Case Title: DEPARTMENT OF INDUSTRIAL RELATIONS VS. BAY AREA RAPID TRANSIT DISTRICT
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Vacate Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, March 26, 2014, Line 5, DEFENDANT BAY AREA RAPID TRANSIT DISTRICT'S (BART) Motion To Vacate Judgment. The motion is granted. The clerk prematurely entered the judgment because whether plaintiff's citation/penalty was properly issued is currently being litigated in Sacramento by a petition for writ of administrative mandamus. Judgment should be entered after defendant's remedies are exhausted. See Labor Code sec. 6650(b). rulings.Case Title: TWIN CITY FIRE INSURANCE COMPANY VS. WELLS FARGO BANK LTD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Deposition Of Pltf, Robin Jeanie Smith
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, March 26, 2014, Line 1. CROSS COMPLAINANT CROWN BUILDING MAINTENANCE, INC'S Motion To Compel Deposition Of Plaintiff Robin Jeanie Smith. Pro Tem Judge James Watson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Grant the unopposed motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jamesw@skwsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CAHILL CONTRACTORS, INC. VS. A&B PAINTING, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IN INTERVENTION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, March 26, 2014, Line 6, DEFENDANT A&B PAINTING, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IN INTERVENTION. The demurrer is overruled in its entirety. St. Paul can maintain its claims because it is standing in the shoes of its insured Cahill. rulings.Case Title: RYAN GARMAN et al VS. UNIVERSITY PIPELINE, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Tom Unger'S Supplemental Responses To Plaintiffs Special Interrogatories To Defendant Tom Unger, Set One, And For Sanctions.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, March 26, 2014, Line 8, PLAINTIFF RYAN GARMAN'S Motion To Compel Defendant Tom Unger'S Supplemental Responses To Plaintiffs Special Interrogatories To Defendant Tom Unger, Set One, And For Sanctions. Plaintiff's motion to compel further responses to Special Interrogatories - Set One is denied. Defendant's objection that the Motion to Compel was untimely filed is overruled. Defendant served his Second Amended Response to Interrogatories during the pendency of an earlier Motion to Compel regarding the same discovery pleading. (See Sinaiko Healthcare Consulting v. Pacific Healthcare Consultants (2007) 8 Cal.App.4th 390. The Court's Order dated January 8, 2014 permitted late service of the Second Amended Response and also directed plaintiff to file a second Motion to Compel if a ruling as to adequacy was needed. Thus, the motion was timely filed within 45 days thereafter. Interrogatories nos. 1, 6-10, 13-17, 20-24, 27-31, 34-39, 41-60, 63-64, 67-68, 76-77, 80-81, 84, and 115 are overbroad, burdensome and oppressive. It is an unreasonable burden and oppressive to require the Defendant to "IDENTIFY" and/or "DESCRIBE" each and every bank transaction for the years requested. (See Mead Reinsurance Co. v. Sup.Ct. (1986) 188 Cal.App.3d 313.) For Interrogatories nos. 117, 119-120, 122-14, 161-163, Defendant has affirmed providing all information to the best of his knowledge and in his direct care, custody and control, directly, or in the form of Electronically Stored Information. Plaintiff's motion to compel Defendant to obtain corporate bank records is moot based on the finding that the underlying inquiries are burdensome and oppressive. Further, the corporate bank records were the subject of subpoenas issued by the Plaintiff, and the records were obtained without objection. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $2,750.00 are awarded in favor of University Pipeline, Inc. and against Plaintiff's counsel on the ground filing of this motion to compel was not justified to be paid within 20 days of service of this Order. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES H LIU MD VS. YORK H LIU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, March 26, 2014, Line 14, CROSS DEFENDANT JAMES LIU'S MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT. The motion is granted. The cause of action for malicious prosecution implicates CCP 425.16 [anti-SLAPP statute]. See Daniels v. Robbins (2010) 182 Cal.App.4th 204, 215 ("The plain language of the anti-SLAPP statute dictates that every claim of malicious prosecution is a cause of action arising from protected activity because every such claim necessarily depends upon written and oral statements in a prior judicial proceeding.") The opposing party (Mr. York Liu) fails to demonstrate a likelihood of prevailing on the merits. Mr. James Liu's "Request for Domestic Violence Restraining Order" was a pure family law matter and it cannot be the basis for a malicious prosecution action based on the public policies highlighted in Bidna v. Rosen (1993) 19 Cal.App.4th 27 and Nicholson v. Fazeli (2003) 113 Cal.App.4th 1091. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: DESHAUN WILLIAMS VS. LIAM BENJAMIN QUINN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Attendance At Deposition Of Pltf And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, March 26, 2014, Line 4. DEFENDANT LIAM QUINN'S Motion To Compel Attendance At Deposition Of Plaintiff And For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge James Watson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Grant the motion. Sanctions of $1,500 are awarded. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jamesw@skwsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
